observations.observation: The experiment needs a visible input surface that makes audience, constraints, decision, and proof objects legible before any deck generation claim appears.
observations.design_rule: Use a cockpit slide to place brief fields, constraints, arm status, and decision pressure in one controlled product surface.
observations.layout_constraint: The slide must reserve one dominant cockpit area with no more than three secondary panels.
observations.qa_signal: Review whether a contact-sheet reader can identify the business brief and blocked publish status without presenter narration.
observations.do_not_copy: Do not copy source visuals, layouts, screenshots, or brand marks.

observations.observation: Tutorial guidance should become a learnable map of evidence, memory, primitive choice, and generated slide behavior rather than a prose checklist.
observations.design_rule: Use a learning map that converts each tutorial rule into a node with an editable output implication.
observations.layout_constraint: The slide must keep the map readable as a single path from intake to generation.
observations.qa_signal: Review whether each map node changes an observable slide primitive or QA rule.
observations.do_not_copy: Do not copy reference templates, glyphs, motion systems, screenshots, or brand marks.

observations.observation: Commercial AI presentation material becomes clearer when technical density is reduced into a visible before-after decision.
observations.design_rule: Use a comparison delta instead of a full table when the audience needs to see how full Vulca differs from prompt-only output.
observations.layout_constraint: The slide must give the primary delta more area than the supporting score details.
observations.qa_signal: Review whether the comparison is understandable from slide thumbnails and whether secondary evidence stays subordinate.
observations.do_not_copy: Do not copy deck visuals, layouts, screenshots, narrative copy, or brand marks.

observations.observation: A credible product workflow treats readiness and review status as part of the product surface rather than as post-hoc notes.
observations.design_rule: Use a QA gate slide that makes render checks, provenance, human review, and public publishing block state explicit.
observations.layout_constraint: The slide must place public publishing blocked status in the main gate area and keep remediation items editable.
observations.qa_signal: Review whether the gate blocks public release until evidence, render, provenance, and human approval exist.
observations.do_not_copy: Do not copy product UI screenshots, article visuals, layouts, or brand marks.

observations.observation: The pack should show that evidence selects the next slide primitive, because a product-lab deck must demonstrate controllable generation rather than a generic aesthetic.
observations.design_rule: Use a decision table to bind evidence type, risk, selected primitive, and next QA action.
observations.layout_constraint: The slide must keep the decision table editable and restrict supporting notes to a narrow side rail.
observations.qa_signal: Review whether each decision row changes either the next slide primitive or the publish gate.
observations.do_not_copy: Do not copy reference visuals, layouts, screenshots, brand systems, or proprietary creative assets.
